7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'

11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {

13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean

15	}

22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),

418	      } else {

557	async function verifyOutputDirectory(directory: string, datamodel: string, schemaPath: string) {
558	  let content: string
559	  try {
560	    content = await fs.readFile(path.join(directory, 'package.json'), 'utf8')
561	  } catch (e) {
562	    if (e.code === 'ENOENT') {
563	      // no package.json exists, we are good
564	      return
565	    }
566	    throw e
567	  }
568	  const { name } = JSON.parse(content)
569	  if (name === clientPkg.name) {
570	    const message = [`Generating client into ${bold(directory)} is not allowed.`]
571	    message.push('This package is used by `prisma generate` and overwriting its content is dangerous.')
572	    message.push('')
573	    message.push('Suggestion:')
574	    const outputDeclaration = findOutputPathDeclaration(datamodel)
575	
576	    if (outputDeclaration && outputDeclaration.content.includes(clientPkg.name)) {
577	      const outputLine = outputDeclaration.content
578	      message.push(`In ${bold(schemaPath)} replace:`)
579	      message.push('')
580	      message.push(`${dim(outputDeclaration.lineNumber)} ${replacePackageName(outputLine, red(clientPkg.name))}`)
581	      message.push('with')
582	
583	      message.push(`${dim(outputDeclaration.lineNumber)} ${replacePackageName(outputLine, green('.prisma/client'))}`)
584	    } else {
585	      message.push(`Generate client into ${bold(replacePackageName(directory, green('.prisma/client')))} instead`)
586	    }
587	
588	    message.push('')
589	    message.push("You won't need to change your imports.")
590	    message.push('Imports from `@prisma/client` will be automatically forwarded to `.prisma/client`')
591	    const error = new Error(message.join('\n'))
592	    throw error
593	  }
594	}

596	function replacePackageName(directoryPath: string, replacement: string): string {
597	  return directoryPath.replace(clientPkg.name, replacement)

600	function findOutputPathDeclaration(datamodel: string): OutputDeclaration | null {
601	  const lines = datamodel.split(/\r?\n/)
602	
603	  for (const [i, line] of lines.entries()) {
604	    if (/output\s*=/.test(line)) {
605	      return { lineNumber: i + 1, content: line.trim() }
606	    }
607	  }
608	  return null
609	}

618	async function copyRuntimeFiles({ from, to, runtimeName, sourceMaps }: CopyRuntimeOptions) {
619	  const files = ['index-browser.js', 'index-browser.d.ts', 'wasm-compiler-edge.js']
620	
621	  files.push(`${runtimeName}.js`)
622	  files.push(`${runtimeName}.d.ts`)

643	/**
644	 * This function ensures that each generated client has unique package name
645	 * It appends sha256 of the schema to the fixed prefix. That ensures unique schemas
646	 * produce unique generated packages while still keeping `generate` results reproducible.
647	 *
648	 * Without unique package name, if you have several TS clients in the project, TS Compiler
649	 * might merge different `Prisma` namespace declarations together and produce unusable results.
650	 *
651	 * @param datamodel
652	 * @returns
653	 */
654	function getUniquePackageName(datamodel: string) {
655	  const hash = createHash('sha256')
656	  hash.write(datamodel)
657	  return `${GENERATED_PACKAGE_NAME_PREFIX}${hash.digest().toString('hex')}`

53	  plugins: [

72	    map(options, (options) => [

74	      () => applyDefaults(options),

220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {

222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled

11	export function parseEnvValue(object: EnvValue) {
12	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
13	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]

16	        `Attempted to load provider value using \`env(${
17	          object.fromEnvVar
18	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
19	          object.fromEnvVar,
20	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
21	      )
22	    }
23	
24	    return value
25	  }
26	
27	  return object.value as string
28	}

37	export function parseBinaryTargetsEnvValue(object: BinaryTargetsEnvValue): string[] | string {
38	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
39	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]

41	      throw new Error(

```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import { BuildOptions } from './build'

type AdapterEntry = {
  entry: string
  outfile: string
}

export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)

    return [
      {
        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
        format: 'cjs' as const,
        bundle: true,
        entryPoints: [entry],
        outfile,
        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
        emitTypes: true,
      },
      {
        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
        format: 'esm' as const,
        bundle: true,
        entryPoints: [entry],
        outfile,
        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
        emitTypes: true,
      },
    ]
  })
}

export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
  {
    name: 'cjs',
    format: 'cjs',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
  {
    name: 'esm',
    format: 'esm',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
]
```

Two build configurations exist:
- **`adapterConfig`**: For bundled single-entry packages
- **`unbundledConfig`**: For multi-file/module packages

```typescript
import type { Generator } from '@prisma/generator'
import type { GeneratorRegistry as IGeneratorRegistry, GeneratorRegistryEntry } from '@prisma/internals'

export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()

  add(generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(generator.name, generator)
  }

  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(name, generator)
  }

  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry {
    return Object.fromEntries(
      [...this.#generators.entries()].map(([name, generator]) => [
        name,
        {
          type: 'in-process',
          generator,
        } satisfies GeneratorRegistryEntry,
      ]),
    )
  }
}
```

### Summary of Key Conventions

1. **Package Naming**: `@prisma/<feature>` (namespace + kebab-case)
2. **Dual Build Output**: All packages emit CJS (`.js`) and ESM (`.mjs`) versions
3. **Type Definitions**: Generated automatically via TypeScript during build
4. **Exports Field**: Structured with `require`/`import` conditions
5. **Build System**: esbuild with custom helpers, supports bundled (adapter) and unbundled (library) modes
6. **Path Mappings**: Centralized in `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` for bundled builds
7. **Workspace Protocol**: Uses `workspace:*` for interdependencies in `package.json`
8. **TypeScript Config**: Base extends `../../tsconfig.json`, build extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`
9. **File Structure**: `src/` source, `dist/` build output, `helpers/build.ts` for build orchestration
10. **Default Scripts**: `dev` (watch mode), `build`, `test` (vitest), `prepublishOnly`

```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import { BuildOptions } from './build'

type AdapterEntry = {
  entry: string
  outfile: string
}

export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)

    return [
      {
        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
        format: 'cjs' as const,
        bundle: true,
        entryPoints: [entry],
        outfile,
        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
        emitTypes: true,
      },
      {
        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
        format: 'esm' as const,
        bundle: true,
        entryPoints: [entry],
        outfile,
        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
        emitTypes: true,
      },
    ]
  })
}

export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
  {
    name: 'cjs',
    format: 'cjs',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
  {
    name: 'esm',
    format: 'esm',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
]
```

Two build configurations exist:
- **`adapterConfig`**: For bundled single-entry packages
- **`unbundledConfig`**: For multi-file/module packages

```typescript
import type { Generator } from '@prisma/generator'
import type { GeneratorRegistry as IGeneratorRegistry, GeneratorRegistryEntry } from '@prisma/internals'

export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()

  add(generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(generator.name, generator)
  }

  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator) {
    this.#generators.set(name, generator)
  }

  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry {
    return Object.fromEntries(
      [...this.#generators.entries()].map(([name, generator]) => [
        name,
        {
          type: 'in-process',
          generator,
        } satisfies GeneratorRegistryEntry,
      ]),
    )
  }
}
```

### Summary of Key Conventions

1. **Package Naming**: `@prisma/<feature>` (namespace + kebab-case)
2. **Dual Build Output**: All packages emit CJS (`.js`) and ESM (`.mjs`) versions
3. **Type Definitions**: Generated automatically via TypeScript during build
4. **Exports Field**: Structured with `require`/`import` conditions
5. **Build System**: esbuild with custom helpers, supports bundled (adapter) and unbundled (library) modes
6. **Path Mappings**: Centralized in `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` for bundled builds
7. **Workspace Protocol**: Uses `workspace:*` for interdependencies in `package.json`
8. **TypeScript Config**: Base extends `../../tsconfig.json`, build extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json`
9. **File Structure**: `src/` source, `dist/` build output, `helpers/build.ts` for build orchestration
10. **Default Scripts**: `dev` (watch mode), `build`, `test` (vitest), `prepublishOnly`

```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs

  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient 
      ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) 
      : '.prisma/client'
    
    return {
      defaultOutput,
      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
      version: clientVersion,
      requiresEngines: [],
      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
    }
  }

  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
    
    await generateClient({
      datamodel: options.datamodel,
      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
      datasources: options.datasources,
      outputDir,
      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
      // ... more config
    })
  }
}
```

13	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]

15	      throw new Error(

17	          object.fromEnvVar
18	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
19	          object.fromEnvVar,

21	      )

Here is my complete implementation plan for `packages/generator-rest-client`:

---

## Overview

**7 new files** + **1 file modified**.

The package is a standalone process-based Prisma generator. When Prisma CLI sees `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"` in a schema, it spawns the compiled binary as a child process and communicates via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stderr. The `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper` handles all protocol plumbing — we only need to implement `onManifest` and `onGenerate`.

---

## Files to Create

### `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`
Standard monorepo layout. Key additions over a plain utility package:
- `"bin": { "prisma-generator-rest-client": "./dist/index.js" }` — makes the package executable as the generator provider
- Dependencies: `@prisma/generator-helper`, `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/internals` (for `parseEnvValue`), `fs-extra` (for `ensureDir`)

### `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`
```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }
```

### `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`
```json
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }
```

### `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`
Uses `createAdapterConfig` (not the pre-baked `adapterConfig`) to inject a `banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` into the CJS output only — this is what makes the built file directly executable as the generator binary when spawned by Prisma CLI.

### `packages/generator-rest-client/src/template.ts`
All string-template helpers:
- `fieldTypeToTs(field)` — maps DMMF `FieldKind`/`type` → TypeScript types (`String→string`, `Int/Float→number`, `DateTime→Date`, `BigInt→bigint`, `Decimal→string`, `Bytes→Uint8Array`, `Json→unknown`; handles `isList` and `!isRequired` wrapping)
- `generateModelInterface(model)` — full read interface (all fields)
- `generateCreateInput(model)` — scalar/enum fields only, optional when `isRequired=false`, excludes auto-generated fields
- `generateUpdateInput(model)` — same but all fields optional
- `generateWhereUniqueInput(model)` — `isId` + `isUnique` scalar fields, all optional
- `generateModelMethods(model)` — the per-model `readonly <name> = { findMany, findUnique, create, update, delete }` block using `fetch`
- `FILE_HEADER` constant — the "do not edit" banner comment

### `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generator.ts`
Orchestrates generation: calls `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` to get the output directory, loops over `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` calling template helpers, builds the full source string, then `ensureDir` + `fs.writeFile('rest-client.ts')`.

### `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts`
Entry point: calls `generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })` where `onManifest` returns `{ prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client Generator', defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client' }` and `onGenerate` delegates to `generate(options)`.

Add one entry to `compilerOptions.paths`:
